title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Browser and Node Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for uris, url resolution, origins, dns, and destination identity, producing destination-resolution and origin contract. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch destination-resolution and origin contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Compare origins from scheme, host, and effective port while separating URI authority, Host, and connection address. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Uri Components Resolution
steps.instruction:
Order the Uri Components Resolution Fetch and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Uri Components Resolution.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Uri Components Resolution assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Uri Components Resolution, keep Uri Components Resolution connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: offline design-system client receives base https://api2.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

content.text: Order work so Uri Components Resolution has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
